mechanism_narrative: An increase in total nonfarm payrolls signals expanding labor income that raises household consumption and corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS increases are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of observed months, or the walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 8 percent signals persistent labor market slack that suppresses wage growth and consumer demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 8 percent, the S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of triggered instances over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous occurrence of unemployment above 7 percent and industrial production below 95 indicates a coordinated contraction in labor and manufacturing activity that amplifies earnings downside, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Instances where UNRATE exceeds 7 percent and INDPRO is below 95 coincide with positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of cases, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a zero interest rate policy environment, an increase in the consumer price index signals successful reflation that lowers real interest rates and boosts nominal corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 0.25 percent, increases in CPIAUCSL coincide with negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55 percent of instances.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in industrial production reflects rising manufacturing output and capacity utilization that supports corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of observed months, or the walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in real gross domestic product signals broad-based expansion in aggregate demand that lifts corporate profit expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 increases are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of observed quarters, or the walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in retail sales signals stronger-than-expected consumer spending momentum that drives near-term earnings revisions and risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS increases are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of observed months, or the walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate of more than 25 basis points signals a surprise monetary tightening that raises discount rates and equity risk premia, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month FEDFUNDS increases greater than 0.25 percentage points coincide with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of triggered instances over any 3-year window.
